title: buxus microphylla var. riparia
intro: Buxus microphylla var. riparia, also known as Japanese boxwood or riparian boxwood, is a compact, evergreen shrub native to Japan, characterized by small leaves and a rounded shape. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in its leaves and seeds, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Buxus microphylla var. riparia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and exhibit antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-parasitic properties.
